{"query":"Artemis II completed April 10 2026 crewed test flight beyond the Moon mission summary official NASA press release","model":"mako","response_time_ms":994,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/mission/artemis-ii/","title":"Artemis II: NASA’s First Crewed Lunar Flyby in 50 Years - NASA","content":"Meet the astronauts who ventured around the Moon on Artemis II, the first crewed flight aboard NASA’s human deep space capabilities, paving the way for future lunar surface missions."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artemis_II","title":"Artemis II - Wikipedia","content":"Artemis II (April 1–11, 2026) was a crewed flyby of the Moon.
It is currently the only crewed flight beyond low Earth orbit since Apollo 17 in 1972.
The SpaceX Falcon Heavy was selected as the launch vehicle.
On February 11, 2023, NASA rotated the Artemis II core stage's engine section to a horizontal position, marking the final major milestone before integration with the rest of the vehicle.
On March 20, the engine section was mated with the core stage in Building 103 at the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans, Louisiana.
In January 2026, NASA administrator Jared Isaacman stated that he supported proceeding with Artemis II using the existing heat shield after reviewing the agency's analysis and meeting with engineers and outside experts.
It was the first crewed launch from LC-39B since STS-116 in 2006.
On flight day 8, a planned manual control test by Wiseman and Glover was canceled so controllers could conduct additional testing of Orion's propulsion system.
The mission also included a new payload titled ARCHeR (Artemis Research for Crew Health & Readiness).
In quarantine, the crew watched Project Hail Mary (2026), which was likened by the media to crewed spaceflight programs like Artemis.

Tomorrow will bring an end to their mission, with a splashdown scheduled for tomorrow (April 10), at 8:07 p.m.
On Tuesday (April 7), a day after Artemis 2's epic flyby of the moon, NASA officials gave us some more news about the mission's famous space toilet.
A few minutes later at 7:07 p.m.
They are due to wake up at 10:50 a.m.
NASA's official livestream will begin at 1 p.m.
EDT (0437 GMT), as Orion flew 39,000 miles (62,764 kilometers) above the moon and 232,000 miles (373,368 km) from Earth.
The Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in lunar space early Monday morning (April 6), becoming the first people to do so since the Apollo 17 crew in December 1972.
The Artemis 2 astronauts are now at Kennedy Space Center's Launch Pad 39B, arriving in their "Astrovan" today (April 1) around 2:13 p.m.
10:30 am ET / T-7:56 to launch: Google Doodle marks Artemis 2 launch day
Today's Google Doodle is dedicated to Artemis 2, highlighting the first crewed mission to circle the moon in over 50 years!
NASA's Artemis 2 Space Launch System rocket has completed its journey from the Vehicle Assembly Building to LC-39B, ending a roughly 10-hour overnight journey.
After more than 11 hours, NASA's might Artemis 2 moon rocket has arrived at it new launch pad home for the first time.

tavily.raw: {"query":"Artemis II completed April 10 2026 crewed test flight beyond the Moon mission summary official NASA press release","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artemis_II","title":"Artemis II - Wikipedia","content":"# Artemis II. 2026 crewed flyby of the Moon. | Names | * Artemis II * Exploration Mission-2 (EM-2) |. **Artemis II** (April 1–11, 2026) was a crewed flyby \"Flyby (spaceflight)\") of the Moon. It was the first crewed flight of the NASA-led Artemis program, the first crewed flight of the Space Launch System (SLS), and the first crewed flight of the Orion spacecraft \"Orion (spacecraft)\"), named ***Integrity*** by the four-person crew. The mission was a test flight supporting the Artemis IV mission to return humans to the lunar surface. The mission's primary goal was to validate the Orion spacecraft \"Orion (spacecraft)\")'s systems, crew operations, and mission procedures ahead of sustained lunar exploration in future Artemis missions. Artemis II's mission objectives were similar to those of Apollo 8 in 1968, the first crewed lunar flight of the Apollo program, while its free-return trajectory resembled that flown by Apollo 13 in 1970.","score":0.83678174,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.planetary.org/articles/artemis-ii-what-to-expect","title":"The Artemis II mission | The Planetary Society","content":"Earth and Moon as imaged from the Orion spacecraft. # The Artemis II mission. NASA's Artemis II mission sent the first humans back to the Moon in over 50 years. A crew of four astronauts undertook a 10-day mission around the Moon and back, kicking off the first crewed mission of the agency's Artemis program, which aims to land people on the Moon in 2028 and eventually set up a base there. > **The mission concluded on April 10, 2026, with a successful splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego.** **See our favorite images from throughout the mission.**. ### The Artemis II crew has left the Orion Capsule. To conclude a very successful mission, the Orion spacecraft carrying the Artemis II crew will splash down into the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego at 8:07 p.m. ET today. Moon and earth from orion. The Artemis II crew has completed the mission’s lunar observation period and is now beginning the return trip back to Earth.","score":0.76173395,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/centers-and-facilities/johnson/artemis-ii-mission-milestones-an-image-and-video-recap","title":"Artemis II Mission Milestones: An Image and Video Recap","content":"Apr 21, 2026 — On April 10, 2026, NASA's Artemis II crew members are hoisted into a U.S. Navy MH-60 helicopter after successfully splashing down in the Pacific ...Read more","score":0.69099766,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.space.com/news/live/artemis-2-nasa-moon-mission-updates-april-10-2026","title":"Artemis 2 Splashdown — NASA hails Orion astronauts' return from moon | Space","content":"# Artemis 2 Splashdown: NASA hails Orion astronauts' return from moon. ## Friday, April 10, 2026: See our latest news and updates on NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission. NASA has successfully launched Artemis 2, its first astronaut mission to the moon since 1972. Liftoff took place on April 1, sending four astronauts on a 10-day trip around the moon aboard the Orion spacecraft. ### NASA hails Orion astronauts' return from moon. NASA&rsquo;s Orion spacecraft is seen as recovery teams work to secure the spacecraft ahead of transferring Artemis II crewmembers. NASA's post-splashdown press briefing has ended and I think it's safe to say that the space agency is over the moon at the amazing success of its Artemis 2 moon mission. Orion and its European Service Module during Artemis I. The second Artemis mission builds on the first, continuing to test the capabilities of the Orion spacecraft and its European Service Module, now fitted with life support systems to keep the crew safe and healthy.
